title: Method for producing enzymes
abstract: The invention relates to a method for producing enzymes, which comprises culturing cells that express one or more types of endogenous or exogenous enzymes in a culture medium and isolating the fraction of extracellular vesicles (EVs) from the cell culture.Enzymes obtainable by the previous method with improved properties and their uses, particularly in enzyme replacement therapy, are also described.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for producing extracellular vesicles (EVs) comprising lysosomal enzymes, characterized in that it comprises the steps of: (a) in vitro culturing, for a certain time and in a cell culture medium, of cells that express one or more types of lysosomal enzymes, wherein said cells are producers of extracellular vesicles (EVs) that are released into the aforementioned culture medium and wherein said EVs are defined by a lipid bilayer with cytosolic liquid in their interior, the culture time being adequate for one or more types of lysosomal enzymes to become expressed in the cell and accumulated in the interior of the extracellular vesicles (EVs); (b) isolating the fraction of extracellular vesicles (EVs) from the culture of stage (a), to obtain isolated EVs comprising one or more types of the lysosomal enzymes expressed in the cells in their interior and/or embedded or associated with said lipid bilayer of these EVs; and (c) optionally, sonicating and/or extruding the fraction of EVs comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes isolated in stage (b). 2. The method, according to claim 1 , characterized in that the lysosomal enzymes that are expressed are selected from the group consisting of: - lysosomal enzymes endogenous to the cultured cells, optionally upon previously subjecting said cells to a genetic editing and/or genetic modification method with an expression vector; - lysosomal enzymes exogenous to the cultured cells, upon previously genetically modifying the cells with an expression vector or through genetic editing; and - combinations of these lysosomal enzymes. 3. The method, according to any one of claims 1 to 2, characterized in that the cells that express one or more types of lysosomal enzymes are transfected animal cells comprising at least one type of expression vector exogenous to the cell which is cultured, said vector comprising a nucleotide sequence that encodes for a lysosomal enzyme. 4. The method, according to any one of claims 1 to 3, characterized in that the cells that express one or more types of lysosomal enzymes are mammal cells. 5. The method, according to any one of claims 1 to 4, characterized in that the 

 isolated cells that express one or more types of lysosomal enzymes are selected from the group consisting of cells derived from Chinese hamster ovary (CHO), dendritic mammal cells, mammal macrophages, and embryonic kidney cells derived from the HEK-293 cell line. 6. The method, according to any one of claims 1 to 5, characterized in that stage (b) of isolating the EV fraction is carried out by means of a method selected from the group consisting of centrifugation, filtration, ultracentrifugation and precipitation, and combinations thereof. 7. The method, according to any one of claims 1 to 6, characterized in that the fraction of EVs that comprise one or more types of lysosomal enzymes that are isolated in stage (b) are EVs with a diameter comprised between 40 and 1 ,000 nm, said diameter being determined by transmission electron microscopy and/or by dynamic light scattering technology. 8. The method, according to any one of claims 1 to 7, characterized in that it comprises stage (c) of sonicating the EVs comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes. 9. The method, according to any one of claims 1-8, characterized in that the lysosomal enzymes are selected from the group consisting of alpha-D-manisodase, N-aspartyl-beta-glucosaminidase, lysosomal acid lipase, cystinosine, protein-2 associated with the lysosomal membrane, alpha-galactosidase A, acid ceramidase, alpha-fucosidase, cathepsin, acid beta-glucosidase, beta-galactosidase, beta- hexosaminidase A, beta-hexosaminidase B, GlcNAc-1 -phosphotransferase, beta- galactosylceramidase, lysosomal acid lipase, arylsulfatase A, alpha-L-iduronidase, iduronate-2-sulfatase, heparan-N-sulfatase, N-acetyl-glucosaminidase, N- acetyltransferase, N-acetylglucosamine 6-sulfatase, N-acetyl galactosamine-6- sulfatase, hyaluronidase, acetyl galactosamine-4-sulfatase, beta-glucuronidase, alpha-N-acetyl neuraminidase, N-acetylinine-1-phosphotransferase, mucolipine-1 , formylglycine-generating enzyme, palmitoyl-protein thioesterase-1 , tripeptidyl- peptidase 1 , cysteine chain protein, CLN3p, CLN5p, CLN6p, CLN7p, CLN8p, CLN8p, acid sphingosine kinase, PC 1 , PC 2, alpha-glucosidase acid, cathepsin K, sialine, alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase, GM2 activator, lysosomal acid lipase and combinations thereof. 10. The method, according to claim 9, characterized in that the lysosomal enzyme is alpha-galactosidase A. 11. The method, according to claim 9, characterized in that the lysosomal enzyme is an enzyme whose deficiency or deficient form is associated with Sanfilippo disease, and which is selected from the group consisting of heparan-N-sulfatase (SGSH), N- acetyl-glucosaminidase (NAGLU), N-acetyltransferase (HGSNAT), N- acetylglucosamine 6-sulfatase (GNS) and combinations thereof. 12. The method, according to any one of claims 1 to 11 , characterized in that the culture time in stage (a) is comprised between 1 and 20 days. 13. The method, according to any one of claims 1 to 12, characterized in that in stage (a) the cells are subjected to culture media and/or culture conditions selected from the group consisting of: hypoxia conditions; radiation with electromagnetic waves; addition of protein expression-stimulating compounds to the culture medium; co cultures with other cells with paracrine effects; and combinations thereof. 14. An isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes obtainable by a method as defined in any one of claims 1 to 13. 15. The isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes according to claim 14, characterized in that the lysosomal enzyme is in the interior and/or embedded in the lipid bilayer of an EV and the percentage by weight of lysosomal enzyme with respect to the total weight of protein in the EV is 1 % (w/w) to 20 % (w/w). 16. The isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes according to claim 14, characterized in that the lysosomal enzyme is embedded in a fragment of the lipid bilayer of an EV, said EV fragment being obtained by disaggregation of the EV. 17. The isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes, according to any of claims 14 to 16, characterized in that the lysosomal is selected from the group consisting of alpha-D-manosidase, N-aspartyl-beta-glucosaminidase, lysosomal acid lipase, cystinosine, protein-2 associated with the lysosomal membrane, alpha-galactosidase A, acid ceramidase, alpha-fucosidase, cathepsin, 

 acid beta-glucosidase, beta-galactosidase, beta-hexosaminidase A, beta- hexosaminidase B, GlcNAc-1 -phosphotransferase, beta-galactosylceramidase, lysosomal acid lipase, arylsulfatase A, alpha-L-iduronidase, iduronate-2-sulfatase, heparan-N-sulfatase, N-acetyl-glucosaminidase, N-acetyltransferase, N- acetylglucosamine 6-sulfatase, N-acetyl galactosamine-6-sulfatase, hyaluronidase, acetyl galactosamine-4-sulfatase, beta-glucuronidase, alpha-N-acetyl neuraminidase, N-acetylinine-1-phosphotransferase, mucolipine-1 , formylglycine-generating enzyme, palmitoyl-protein thioesterase-1 , tripeptidyl-peptidase 1 , cysteine chain protein, CLN3p, CLN5p, CLN6p, CLN7p, CLN8p, CLN8p, acid sphingosine kinase, PC 1 , PC 2, alpha-glucosidase acid, cathepsin K, sialine, alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase, GM2 activator, lysosomal acid lipase and combinations thereof. 18. An isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes, as defined in any one of claims 14 to 17, for use as a medicament. 19. The isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes for use according to claim 18, characterized in that it is for use in the treatment of lysosomal storage diseases. 20. The isolated EV for use according to claim 19, characterized in that the lysosomal storage diseases are selected from the group consisting of Alpha-manosidosis, aspartylglucosaminuria, cholesteryl ester storage disease, cystinosis, Danon disease, Fabry disease, Farber disease, fucosidosis, galactosialidosis, Gaucher disease Type I, Gaucher disease Type II, Gaucher disease Type III, Gaucher Gangliosidosis Type I, Gaucher disease, Gangliosidosis GMI Type III, GM2 - Sandhoff disease, GM2 - Tay-Sachs disease, GM2 - Gangliosidosis, AB variant, Mucolipidosis II, Krabbe disease, Lysosomal acid lipase deficiency, MPS I - Schein, Leucodystrophy, MPS I Hurler-Scheie Syndrome, MPS II - Hunter Syndrome, MPS IIIA - Sanfilippo Syndrome Type A, MPS NIB - Sanfilippo Syndrome Type B, MPS NIB - Sanfilippo Syndrome Type C, MPS NIB - Sanfilippo Syndrome Type D, MPS IV - Morquio Type A, MPS IV - Morquio Type B, MPS IX - Hyaluronidase deficiency, MPS VI - Maroteaux-Lamy, MPS VII - Sly Syndrome, Mucolipidosis I - Sialidosis, Mucolipidosis NIC, Mucolipidosis Type IV, Multiple sulfatase deficiency, Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis T2, Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis T3, Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis T4, Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis T5, Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis T6, Mild Neuronal Ceroid apparent in the person making the inspections , Pompe disease, Pycnodysostosis, Salla disease, Schindler disease, Wolman disease and combinations thereof. 21. The isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes, for use according to claim 20, characterized in that the lysosomal storage disease is Fabry disease. 22. The isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes, for use according to claim 20, characterized in that the lysosomal storage disease is a Sanfilippo Syndrome selected from MPSII - Hunter's Syndrome, MPS IIIA - Sanfilippo Syndrome Type A, MPS NIB - Sanfilippo Syndrome Type B, MPS NIB - Sanfilippo Syndrome Type C, MPS NIB - Sanfilippo Syndrome Type D. 23. The isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes for use according to any claims 18 to 22, characterized in that the treatment is an autologous treatment, wherein the EV that comprises the lysosomal enzyme is isolated from the cells of a patient who will be receiving the treatment. 24. The isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes for use according to any claims 18 to 22, characterized in that the treatment is a heterologous treatment, wherein the EV that comprises the lysosomal enzyme is isolated from cells of a patient different from the patient that will be receiving the treatment. 25. A pharmaceutical composition comprising an isolated EV comprising one or more types of lysosomal enzymes, as defined in any one of claims 14 to 17, in therapeutically effective amounts, together with pharmaceutically acceptable excipients or carriers.
